author: maintainer-agent

patch.value:
## Motivation
The composability story (pm-m9xv) promises arbitrary on-the-fly queries via pm + grep/jq/xargs. Today fixed filter flags cover common axes and inline field:value tokens exist in search, but any predicate combining OR/NOT or comparing numeric/date fields forces: pm list-all --json | jq '<predicate>' — a full workspace scan streamed to the client. The scale story (pm-g2zz) requires bounded token output regardless of workspace size, so complex selection must move server-side.

## Direction
- Extend the existing inline field:value token mini-language (already parsed by search) rather than inventing a second syntax; add grouping, OR/NOT, and comparators.
- Compile the predicate to index lookups where the index has the field, falling back to per-candidate evaluation only within index-narrowed candidates.
- Expose the parsed predicate AST in the SDK so integrators (pm-w89s) and non-PM domain builders (pm-8ngt) get the same query power programmatically.

## Sequencing
After pm-801d (index) and pm-dfg0 (pagination); grammar placement after ADR pm-pbyu acceptance. Horizon: long (with the rest of pm-9rxu implementation).
Index-backed query predicates: --where expression on list/search so complex selections stay O(result) at million-item scale

Complex boolean/comparator selections currently require streaming full --json dumps through jq; at 100k-1M items that breaks the bounded-output contract. Evaluate predicate expressions inside the read path against the persistent index.

pm list and pm search accept a predicate expression (--where or extended inline field:value mini-language) supporting AND/OR/NOT grouping, comparison operators over built-in and custom fields (priority<=1, estimated_minutes>60, updated_at ranges), and tag membership; evaluation happens inside the read path against the persistent derived index (pm-801d) with cursor pagination (pm-dfg0) so output stays O(result); works with --format ndjson for downstream jq/grep; the same predicate primitive is exposed in the public SDK query API; parse errors are agent-friendly (position + expected tokens); no new top-level noun — flag placement per ADR pm-pbyu; grammar documented and contract-listed.

message: Filed from 2026-07-07 all-status backlog review: composability-at-scale gap

author: maintainer-agent
message: Sequence behind index+pagination; grammar placement per ADR
